## How to Contribute

### Reporting Issues

- Search existing issues before creating a new one
- Use a clear, descriptive title
- Include steps to reproduce, expected behavior, and actual behavior
- Add relevant labels if possible

### Submitting Changes

1. Create a branch from `main` (or the default branch): [TODO: define branch naming convention, e.g., `feature/description`, `fix/description`]
2. Make your changes with clear, incremental commits
3. Follow the commit message convention: [TODO: define convention, e.g., Conventional Commits]
4. Ensure all tests pass before submitting
5. Open a Pull Request with a clear description of what changed and why

### Pull Request Guidelines

- Link the PR to the related issue or work item
- Keep PRs focused and reasonably sized
- Ensure the PR description answers: What changed? Why?
- Request review from at least [TODO: N] team member(s)
